reforms.description: The Spellings Commission proposed an Action Plan to improve higher education in the United States, emphasizing the need for a federal research agenda for adult literacy and calling for a substantial increase in needs-based financial grants, specifically aiming to raise the purchasing power of the average Pell Grant to 70% of the average in-state tuition at public four-year institutions over five years.
reforms.component_name: Federal Research Agenda and Pell Grant Increase
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to higher education and adult literacy is expected to enhance human capital and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were proposed by the Spellings Commission and have not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed actions represent a significant overhaul of funding and strategic direction in higher education.
reforms.source_quote: The Commission found that 'UShigher education needs to improve in dramatic ways' changing from 'a system primarily based on reputation to one based on performance.'

reforms.description: The government is promoting the use of health information technology to enhance the care delivery system, including the establishment of an interoperable health information network to improve healthcare choices and cost-conscious spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing health information technology is expected to improve efficiency and reduce costs in healthcare delivery.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been proposed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant investment in health infrastructure but is not a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government is promoting the use of health information technology to enhance the care delivery system, including the availability of price and quality information to individuals.

reforms.description: The President’s Tax Reform Panel recommended reducing the deductibility of mortgage interest expense from the taxable income base and replacing it with a targeted tax credit for homeowners, aimed at promoting homeownership while reducing tax benefits skewed towards higher income households.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While promoting homeownership can stimulate economic activity, the reduction in mortgage interest deductibility may create disincentives for some homeowners.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendations were made by the President’s Tax Reform Panel but have not yet been legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in tax policy affecting a large segment of the population.
reforms.source_quote: The Panel recommended that the existing deduction be replaced with a better targeted tax expenditure, a tax credit available to all homeowners.
